methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Dendra Systems is a Series A-stage climate and energy company co-founded by Susan Graham and Irina Fedorenko that has raised $18M to date. Valuation analysis relies on two primary methods applied against a reference universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA), all operating in the Climate & Energy sector at similar stages.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $36M based on the $18M raised proxy. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—produces a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. These two methods diverge materially, reflecting the wide dispersion in early-stage climate-tech valuations.

Triangulation of both approaches supports a valuation range of $31M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $93M. The wide spread underscores two key uncertainties: first, the relative maturity and traction of peer companies versus Dendra's current operational status remains unclear; second, the composition of that 9-company private peer set is not fully detailed, limiting confidence in the median anchor. Near-term revenue metrics, customer pipeline depth, and technology differentiation relative to the named peer set would narrow this range materially.
